back: A storage layer that keeps frequently accessed data in faster storage for quicker retrieval.
A request that finds the desired data in the cache.
A request where the requested data is not found in cache and must be fetched elsewhere.
The percentage of requests served directly from cache.
Time To Live specifies how long a cache entry remains valid before expiring.
The process of removing entries from cache when space is needed.

front: What is the LRU eviction policy?
back: Least Recently Used removes the item that has not been accessed for the longest time.

front: What is the LFU eviction policy?
back: Least Frequently Used removes items with the lowest access frequency.
Writes are stored in cache first and flushed to persistent storage later.
Cached data can become stale, requiring careful coordination with underlying storage.
Many requests simultaneously miss cache and overwhelm the backend system.
Preloading frequently accessed data into cache before traffic arrives.
A cache spread across multiple machines to increase capacity and availability.
The degree to which cached data matches the authoritative source of truth.
The insertion of malicious or incorrect content into a cache.
Cache sharding distributes keys across multiple cache nodes to increase capacity and throughput.
A cache that automatically loads missing data from the backing store when a cache miss occurs.
A strategy where cache entries are proactively refreshed before they expire.
A local cache located close to the application that reduces access latency to frequently used data.
The guarantee that multiple caches maintain a consistent view of shared data.
